picks.name: Leidos Holdings, Inc.
picks.thesis: LDOS is down 48% from its 52-week high while earnings grew 28%/yr — a sentiment-driven pullback, not fundamental deterioration. Quality looks intact (healthy margins, durable revenue, long-term uptrend), and it's now at or below fair value ($106.11 vs $214.63, medium confidence). Valued with DCF and Comps.

methods.name: DDM — dividend discount
methods.reason: Not meaningful: this is a growth-oriented company that reinvests and returns cash mainly via buybacks rather than dividends (yield 1.5%, payout 15%), so a dividend-only model understates it — DDM excluded

reconciliation.sentence: The methods land between $117.13 and $312.12 (a 166% spread). They broadly agree on direction but differ on magnitude — medium confidence.

why: medium confidence across 2 models — DCF $117.13, Comps $312.12
profile: ✓ → multiple compression: price down 48% while earnings grew +28%/yr (sentiment, not deterioration)
